title: MIH Website
description: A high-performance frontend implementation for the MIH platform focusing on modular design and speed.
fullDescription: The MIH Website project involved building a scalable, accessible frontend architecture using Next.js. I designed a 'Lego‑style' component system that enabled rapid page assembly and consistent branding across the entire site. The implementation achieved a 95+ Lighthouse performance score through aggressive code splitting, optimal image loading strategies, and Tailwind CSS utility optimisation. I also integrated Framer Motion for micro‑interactions and page transitions, creating an engaging user experience while maintaining a zero‑runtime CSS approach for critical rendering paths.

title: 2026 Hyundai Sonata vs. 2026 Nissan Altima Page Creation
description: Full page creation for a regional automotive comparison landing page targeting Cuyahoga Falls & Akron, Ohio.
fullDescription: I created the complete landing page comparing the 2026 Hyundai Sonata and 2026 Nissan Altima, from initial content planning to final deployment. The work included researching high-intent local keywords like '2026 Sonata vs Altima Cuyahoga Falls', mapping competitor content gaps, and writing over 1,500 words of original, regionally-tailored copy. I built the page structure with clear heading hierarchy, comparison tables, and call-to-action sections. On the technical side, I implemented Vehicle and AggregateOffer schema markup, optimised all images with WebP and lazy loading, and inline critical CSS to achieve a 30% reduction in Largest Contentful Paint. I also crafted location-specific meta titles and descriptions to improve visibility in the Akron–Canton area, and set up internal links to dealership inventory pages. The result was a high-performing, conversion-ready page that significantly increased organic visibility for long-tail local queries.

title: 2026 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 vs. 2026 Toyota Tundra
description: Data-driven page creation for a high-intent truck comparison, built for maximum mobile performance.
fullDescription: I took ownership of creating the Silverado 1500 vs. Tundra comparison page from the ground up, focusing on both engaging content and exceptional Core Web Vitals. After a deep dive into user intent and competitor page analysis, I structured the page with a logical flow: hero section with a quick winner summary, detailed side‑by‑side specs, towing and payload comparisons, and a FAQ section using FAQ schema. I wrote all copy, emphasising key differentiators that local truck buyers care about. For performance, I identified oversized images as the main bottleneck, replaced them with modern WebP formats at correct resolutions, and applied lazy loading throughout. I inlined critical CSS and deferred non‑essential scripts, raising the mobile Lighthouse score to 92. I also implemented internal links to relevant inventory and financing pages to capture high‑intent commercial traffic, turning the page into an effective lead‑generation asset.

title: SyncSnap
description: A modern web application facilitating instant synchronization and data sharing for technical teams.
fullDescription: SyncSnap is a real‑time collaboration platform designed for technical documentation teams. As the frontend lead, I built the UI with React and TypeScript, leveraging Socket.io for live data synchronisation across multiple clients. I implemented optimistic UI updates and state reconciliation to ensure a seamless user experience even during network latency. The architecture follows a modular component pattern with Tailwind CSS for styling, achieving a 100% Lighthouse accessibility score. Key features include inline commenting, version history visualisation, and a multi‑user presence system. Performance was optimised through selective rendering, memoisation, and efficient WebSocket subscription management.

title: Sibol
description: A smart farming assistance platform designed to help farmers manage agricultural activities digitally.
fullDescription: Sibol on Base is a smart farming assistance platform built to support farmers in managing daily agricultural operations through a centralized and user-friendly system. The platform allows users to monitor crop progress, manage farming tasks, record harvests and expenses, and organize farm-related information efficiently. Developed using modern web technologies, the system focuses on improving productivity, accessibility, and decision-making for farmers and agricultural communities. Sibol aims to promote technology-driven farming by providing a simple, organized, and scalable digital solution for modern agriculture.

title: 2026 Ford Ranger - Danville, Kentucky Model Page
description: End-to-end page creation for the 2026 Ford Ranger launch in the Danville, Kentucky market.
fullDescription: I built and optimised the complete model page for the 2026 Ford Ranger targeting Danville, Kentucky. The page creation process started with local keyword research, uncovering high‑opportunity phrases like 'new Ford Ranger Danville' and '2026 Ranger for sale KY'. I crafted all on‑page content — including trim-level overviews, feature highlights, and a customised FAQ section — with local relevance at its core. To support future updates, I designed a modular content blueprint that could easily accommodate new specs and inventory feeds. I implemented Vehicle schema markup and local business structured data to strengthen visibility in Google's local pack, and added canonical tags to prevent duplicate content issues. I also optimised the page for crawl efficiency with a clean, flat URL structure and breadcrumb navigation. The final page launched with a projected 25% increase in organic traffic for the Danville area, driven by its strong technical foundation and locally‑tuned content.

title: FlowState
description: A minimalist productivity dashboard designed to keep developers in the 'zone'.
fullDescription: FlowState is a deeply minimal productivity dashboard that reduces cognitive load for developers. I built the entire frontend with Next.js, Node.js, and TypeScript. The application includes a custom task manager with drag‑and‑drop ordering, a Pomodoro timer, and an ambient sound generator with Web Audio API. To maintain a perfect 100/100 Lighthouse score, I employed extreme code splitting, dynamic imports, and a strict atomic CSS strategy via Tailwind. The UI follows a monochromatic palette with subtle blur effects to minimise visual noise. User settings are persisted locally using IndexedDB, and all animations respect prefers‑reduced‑motion. This project refined my skills in performance budgeting and accessibility‑first design.

title: LERA: Learning Review Application
description: An application for reviewers with flashcard sets and practice tests.
fullDescription: LERA is a comprehensive learning review application designed to help users prepare for exams and tests. It features customizable flashcard sets, practice tests with different question formats, and progress tracking. The backend is built with Laravel and PHP, while the mobile application is developed using React Native and Expo.

title: StoryTold
fullDescription: StoryTold is a mobile application that allows users to create and share stories. It is built with React Native and Expo, and it uses Firebase for its backend services, including authentication and data storage.

title: IRIS
description: A web application for resort and hotel management.
fullDescription: IRIS is a web application for managing resort and hotel reservations and operations. It is built with PHP and Laravel, and it uses MySQL for its database. The system helps manage bookings, customer information, and room availability.

title: Resortvation
description: A UI/UX design for a resort reservation system.
fullDescription: Resortvation is a UI/UX design project for a resort reservation system. The design was created in Figma and focuses on providing a user-friendly and intuitive booking experience. It includes wireframes, mockups, and prototypes for the entire reservation flow.
